assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: The response clearly explains the 'double jeopardy' concept - spot VMs face both normal upgrade disruption (cordoning and draining) and potential spot interruption from cloud provider

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: The response mentions spot-tolerant workloads and provides tolerations configuration for workloads that can handle preemption

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: The response shows maxSurge: 2 in the example but doesn't specifically recommend higher maxSurge values for spot pools due to their fault-tolerant nature

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: The response shows maxUnavailable: 0 in the example, which contradicts this expectation. It doesn't advise setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: The response does not mention potential issues with spot VM availability affecting the ability to create surge nodes during upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: The response provides a PodDisruptionBudget example and emphasizes ensuring proper PDBs as part of pre-upgrade preparation

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: The response shows YAML configuration for surge settings but does not provide the specific gcloud command for configuring surge settings on a spot pool

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: The response actually suggests the opposite - upgrading on-demand nodes first, then spot nodes, stating 'Upgrade on-demand nodes first (more stable)'
